message:
feat(termwiz/widgets): add render telemetry and dirty-rect-aware composition

Instrument the widget rendering pipeline with per-frame telemetry that
tracks dirty rectangle counts and cell coverage at both the per-widget
and composed-frame levels:

RenderTelemetry struct:
- widgets_rendered: count of widgets processed in the render pass
- widget_dirty_rects / widget_dirty_cells: aggregate dirty region metrics
  accumulated during per-widget draw_from_screen_with_dirty_rects() calls
- frame_dirty_rects / frame_dirty_cells: dirty region metrics from the
  diff between the previous composed frame and the current alt_screen

Ui changes:
- Add last_render_telemetry field and public accessor for post-render
  inspection by callers that need upload budgeting or performance monitoring
- Add accumulate_dirty_rects() helper that folds a &[DirtyRect] slice
  into (count, total_cells) using saturating arithmetic
- Replace screen.draw_from_screen() with draw_from_screen_with_dirty_rects()
  in render_recursive(), threading a &mut RenderTelemetry through the
  recursive widget tree traversal
- Add render_to_screen_with_dirty_rects() returning (bool, Vec<DirtyRect>)
  that computes frame-level dirty regions via screen.dirty_rects(&alt_screen)
- Preserve the existing render_to_screen() API as a thin wrapper that
  discards the dirty rect vector for backward compatibility

Tests:
- render_telemetry_tracks_dirty_rects: verifies widget counts, dirty rect
  presence on first render, and zero frame-level dirtiness on idempotent
  second render
- render_to_screen_with_dirty_rects_reports_frame_regions: verifies the
  new API returns non-empty dirty rects on initial render

This is the widget-layer complement to the Surface::dirty_rects() and
DirtyRect infrastructure added in the surface crate, enabling end-to-end
partial-screen-update budgeting from widget render through GPU upload.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
